trans-unit.source: QGIS is open source and therefore freely available to everyone at no cost. You can install QGIS for Windows, Mac and Linux computers.
trans-unit.target: QGIS is open source and therefore freely available to everyone at no cost. You can install QGIS for Windows, Mac and Linux computers.

trans-unit.source: Generally, we recommend using the latest Long Term Release, because it is the most stable and contains the fewest bugs.
trans-unit.target: Generally, we recommend using the latest Long Term Release, because it is the most stable and contains the fewest bugs.

trans-unit.source: You can download the latest version here:
trans-unit.target: You can download the latest version here:

trans-unit.source: Select `Download for Windows`, `Download for macOS` or `Download for Linux`, depending on your system.
trans-unit.target: Select `Download for Windows`, `Download for macOS` or `Download for Linux`, depending on your system.

trans-unit.source: Click on `Looking for the most stable version? Get QGIS 3.28 LTR`.
trans-unit.target: Click on `Looking for the most stable version? Get QGIS 3.28 LTR`.

trans-unit.source: For __Windows operating systems__, there is always a 32-bit version and a 64-bit version of each QGIS version available for download. Which version to install depends on your computer and operating system. If it is not clear how many bits your operating system has, you can easily find out: Left-click on the __Windows icon at__ the bottom left of the screen (alternatively, open the Windows search function). Type __"System"__ on the keyboard, click on the entry __"System"__ in the search results. Under the item __"System type"__ you can read the bit number.
trans-unit.target: For __Windows operating systems__, there is always a 32-bit version and a 64-bit version of each QGIS version available for download. Which version to install depends on your computer and operating system. If it is not clear how many bits your operating system has, you can easily find out: Left-click on the __Windows icon at__ the bottom left of the screen (alternatively, open the Windows search function). Type __"System"__ on the keyboard, click on the entry __"System"__ in the search results. Under the item __"System type"__ you can read the bit number.

trans-unit.source: You can install QGIS on Linux system through `apt` using:
trans-unit.target: You can install QGIS on Linux system through `apt` using:
